---
title: "CA_lab"
---

## Quarto

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>.

## Setup

```{r}
# libraries used
library(data.table)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
```

## Install Plink

```{r}

# checks if plink is installed
system("C:/Users/emedi/PLINK/plink.exe --version")

```

## Install bigsnpr

```{r}
# bigsnpr install
#install.packages("bigsnpr")
library(bigsnpr)
library(bigstatsr)
# run line below only one time ever
#snp_readBed("data/penncath.bed") 
```

## Looking at Data

```{r}
# looking at data
head(fam <- fread('data/penncath.fam'))
(clinical <- fread('data/penncath.csv'))
bim <- fread('data/penncath.bim')
head(bim)
penncath <- snp_attach("data/penncath.rds")
str(penncath)

```

## Quality Control

We notice here that (1) we do not have any sex chromosomes[2](https://pbreheny.github.io/adv-gwas-tutorial/quality_control.html#fn2), and (2) we do not have any chromosomes with numbers outside 1-23. Sometimes, SNPs with unknown chromosome are labeled with a chromosome of 0.

```{r}
# Quality Control
penncath$map$chromosome |> table()
```

## Missing Data

The summary is the statistics of SNP's with missing samples. We can see that the max amount missing is 1401.

```{r}
class(penncath$genotypes)
# this line gives documenation on 
#?bigstatsr::big_counts # handy function to summarize data 
snp_stats <- big_counts(penncath$genotypes)
dim(snp_stats) # 4th row has NA counts 
boxplot(snp_stats[4,]) 
summary(snp_stats[4,])
```

We then look at samples that have high proportions of high values. Through the boxplot, we can see that there are no outliers that have a high proportion of missing values.

```{r}
# summarize genotype data by *sample* 
sample_stats <- big_counts(penncath$genotypes, byrow = TRUE)
sample_stats[, 1:10]
boxplot(sample_stats[4,])
summary(sample_stats[4,])
```

If an individual had a ton of A/B calls but no A/A or B/B calls, or vice versa, that would likely indicate something was wrong in that sample – we would typically expect all samples to have some heterozygous calls and some homozygous calls. We see that the distribution is somewhat normal and that there are no big outliers in our dataset.

```{r}
# heterozygosity check
allele_dat <- sweep(x = sample_stats[1:3,], 
                    # ^ leave off 4th row -- don't count NAs here
                    MARGIN = 1,
                    STATS = c(0, 1, 0),
                    FUN = "*") |> colSums()

boxplot(allele_dat/ncol(snp_stats))
hist(allele_dat/ncol(snp_stats),
     main = "Zygosity",
     xlab = "Proportion of samples which are heterozygous") # should be bell-curved shaped

```

## Minor Allele Frequency (MAF) filtering

We look at SNP's that have minor alleles that occur very rarely. This histogram shows that there are some SNP's that have these minor alleles. These minor variants will need to be filtered out later. Since mating is not random, and thus, violates the Hardy Weinberg principle, we use a different criteria. We will filter the data based off of a binomial distribution instead.

```{r}
hist(snp_stats[1,])
summary(snp_stats[1,])
```

## Implementation of QC

Here are some basic steps that should be followed for quality control 1) check the chromosomes we have represented in our data

2)  filter out samples that have a lot of missing values (e.g., samples that had poor quality in upstream data collection/analysis)

3)  check for heterozygosity, and filter out samples that are clear outliers

4)  filter out SNPs that have a really low minor allele frequency

5)  filter out SNPs that are far outside Hardy-Weinberg equilibrium

6)  consider relatedness in our data – both the relatedness that is known as well as any latent relationship structure(s).

```{r}
# preps data files for
system("C:/Users/emedi/PLINK/plink --bfile data/penncath --make-bed --out data/penncath_clean")

```

```{r}
# if code below has been run before run this
  #file.remove("data/qc_penncath.bed","data/qc_penncath.bim","data/qc_penncath.fam", "data/qc_penncath.bk")
```

```{r}
path_to_qc_penncath_bed <- snp_plinkQC(
  plink.path = "C:/Users/emedi/PLINK/plink", # again, you may need to change this according to your machine!
  prefix.in = "data/penncath_clean", # input data
  prefix.out = "data/qc_penncath", # creates *new* rds with quality-controlled data
  maf = 0.01, # filter out SNPs with MAF < 0.01
  geno = 0.1, # filter out SNPs missing more than 10% of data
  mind = 0.1, # filter out SNPs missing more than 10% of data,
  hwe = 1e-10, # filter out SNPs that have p-vals below this threshold for HWE test
  autosome.only = TRUE # we want chromosomes 1-22 only
  
)
```

```{r}
qc_file_path <- snp_readBed(bedfile = path_to_qc_penncath_bed)

qc <- snp_attach(qc_file_path)

dim(qc$genotypes)
```
